authoritative_sources.title: GS1 US Best Practice Guideline for Exchanging Product Images and Attributes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US apparel and general merchandise guidance is a recognized public reference for how product images and attributes are created, managed, and supplied across retail operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request intake, sample identification, and review-context setup
used_for: style, SKU, color, size, and image attribute baselines
evidence expectations for visual sample requests

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 US Guideline for Exchanging Raw Material Attributes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US raw-material guidance is a recognized apparel-industry reference for standardized material vocabulary and identifying like-kind materials for sourcing and specification communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sample identification and unsupported substitution boundary review
used_for: request packaging for sourcing or supplier-quality escalation

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 US Guideline for Floor-Ready Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US floor-ready merchandise guidance is a recognized public operating reference for apparel and general merchandise presentation, packaging, and logistics expectations used downstream of sample review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sample pull preparation, showroom or studio handoff, and return readiness

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Package and Product Measurement Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GS1 Package and Product Measurement Standard is the authoritative public standard for repeatable product and package measurements, including default-front and dimensional-capture rules in shared trade-item data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sample setup, attribute verification, and comparison of sample variants
used_for: evidence discipline when samples differ by dimensional attributes

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 General Specifications are the core public standard for barcode and identification-key use across trade items and logistics units.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sample check-in, check-out, and logistics-event evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: GTIN Management Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GTIN Management Standard is the authoritative public baseline for when a changed trade item should be treated as a new identity in open supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: variant review and sample identity change handling
used_for: new versus prior sample identity logic
refusal to merge materially changed samples into the same identity without evidence

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's global traceability standard is the authoritative public baseline for identifying, capturing, and sharing traceability data across supply-chain events.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reservation, custody transfer, courier handoff, and return reconciliation
used_for: authoritative record expectations for location disputes
blocked progression when custody evidence conflicts

authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC care-label guidance is authoritative U.S. public guidance for when care labels must be attached and what evidence supports care warnings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: compliance boundary review for final-sample requests
used_for: routing when label evidence is requested as final signoff

authoritative_sources.title: Tracking Label Business Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC tracking-label guidance is authoritative U.S. public guidance for permanent product and packaging identifiers, including manufacturer or importer, production place and date, and batch or run details needed to trace affected children's products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality hold review, traceability exception handling, and safety-boundary escalation
used_for: traceability evidence expectations for product and packaging marks
refusal to treat safety-significant samples as routine pulls

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC recall guidance is the authoritative operating reference for stopping production, isolating inventory, notifying the distribution chain, and distinguishing reworked products from defective products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold, blocked release, and recall-related sample isolation
used_for: refusal to release held or recalled samples for routine use

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 9001 Auditing Practices Group: Nonconformity - review and closing
authoritative_sources.publisher: ISO Technical Committee 176 and International Accreditation Forum
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ISO 9001 Auditing Practices Group is a recognized public quality-management reference for correction, cause analysis, corrective action, and objective evidence before a nonconformity is closed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: damaged sample review, corrective action tracking, and closure evidence
used_for: difference between containment, correction, and corrective action
completion evidence standards for damaged or held samples

workflow_stages.name: request intake and sample identification review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request purpose, style or SKU identity, color and size variant, sample type, due date, and destination context before a pull is accepted.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: reservation, pull, and custody assignment
workflow_stages.description: Reserve the right sample, package the pull, record current holder and location, and block progression if the sample cannot be tied to authoritative custody evidence.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: check-out, shipment, and review handoff management
workflow_stages.description: Move samples into review, showroom, photo-studio, or external courier flow only when the handoff records, due dates, and return expectations are explicit.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception handling, quality hold, and unsupported approval routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing samples, conflicting pulls, damaged samples, missing tracking-label or batch-run evidence, safety or compliance concerns, unsupported substitutions, and requests that require quality, compliance, engineering, or supplier authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: return, check-in, disposition, and closure evidence
workflow_stages.description: Close the request only when return, location, receipt, or disposition evidence is complete and no open hold, recall, or nonconformity posture remains unresolved.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: sample requests, style and SKU sheets, color and size references, and request-purpose notes
fit comments, technical design notes, showroom request records, and sales or photo-shoot pull lists
sample-room reservation logs, check-out and check-in logs, courier shipment records, and return or pull sheets
sample location trackers, holder assignments, due-date records, and overdue-return logs
quality hold notes, damage logs, tracking-label details, batch or run identifiers, recall notices, disposition records, corrective-action notes, and destruction or rework authorizations
supplier or vendor references, substitution requests, and sourcing escalation notes
completion records showing return, receipt, or final disposition support

allowed: classify sample-room work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and public-source context required to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, escalation notes, and completion records without overstating approval authority
escalate: final fit, technical design, engineering, grading, or production-readiness approval
final compliance, care-label, tracking-label, children's-product, or legal interpretation
supplier approval, material substitution approval, or supplier-quality disposition
formal quality release, recall disposition, or hold override
refuse: pull, ship, or close sample work when the style, sample identity, location, or return evidence is missing and would change the outcome
release held, damaged, or recall-linked samples for use without formal quality or compliance direction
bypass traceability, hold, release, or quality controls to meet review, studio, or sales deadlines

domain_failure_modes: sample identity is confused because style, SKU, color, size, tracking-label, or batch-run records are incomplete or inconsistent across systems
a sample is declared available even though custody records show it is checked out, in transit, or overdue for return
multiple teams receive conflicting promises for the same sample because priority rules and reservation controls are not applied
a held, damaged, or recall-linked sample is released for marketing or review without formal quality or compliance direction
a supplier or material substitution is treated as routine sample handling instead of a sourcing or supplier-quality decision
sample-room staff are pushed into final fit, compliance, or technical approval instead of preserving boundary-safe escalation
closure is recorded without authoritative return, receipt, or disposition evidence
tenant terminology masks missing baseline evidence or hides unresolved ownership and hold posture

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
A critical sample cannot be located when a review or shoot is due
Multiple teams request the same sample with conflicting deadlines

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources support apparel traceability, data, and boundary handling, but they do not standardize local sample classes such as proto, SMS, PPS, salesman sample, or archive pull. Tenants must supply their own taxonomy and priority meanings at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific labeling overlays beyond the U.S. public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: This validated baseline uses FTC and CPSC public sources for U.S. labeling and product-safety boundaries. Tenants operating in additional jurisdictions must supply the relevant local apparel-labeling and product-safety overlays.

notes: Authoritative sample records are the PLM, sample-room inventory tracker, courier log, and showroom request records designated by the tenant, constrained by GS1 traceability, FTC labeling, CPSC hold or recall, and ISO nonconformity evidence baselines where relevant.

The lane stops before final fit approval, technical or engineering signoff, supplier approval, quality release, and regulatory or legal labeling interpretation even when a high-priority review or shoot is pending.
